scenario_results.reason: Produced an API surface boundary map and path-by-path design tied to requirements, consumers, domain glossary, integration constraints, idempotent writes, and assumptions without taking implementation ownership.

Returned OpenAPI-first specification recommendations with components, schemas, parameters, examples, security schemes, version notes, and contract-test evidence while requiring schema and version-policy clarity.
Identified breaking changes, client impact, compatibility-safe alternatives, migration and deprecation steps, telemetry checks, test evidence, rollback plan, and final approval boundary.
Handled vague requirements as low confidence, listed missing tenant facts and guardrails, and avoided invented schemas, legal retention, privacy behavior, or compatibility commitments.

Mapped retries, duplicate submissions, stale updates, malformed input, authorization failure, quota exhaustion, and upstream failures to HTTP semantics, Problem Details, idempotency, concurrency, redaction, and tests.

Recommended stable cursor semantics, deterministic ordering, filter and sort contracts, invalid query errors, conditional request considerations, examples, and tests while routing database execution out of lane.
Identified API contract-shape risks using OWASP API Security and NIST SSDF concepts and routed threat model ownership, risk acceptance, and security policy decisions to security owners.
Blocked unsupported closure when schemas, OpenAPI diff, contract tests, compatibility records, gateway limits, and reviewer responses were missing or contradictory.
Produced a remediation note with severity, owner, contract changes, example updates, tests, resubmission checklist, and closure criteria for open API findings.
Refused frontend and database implementation ownership, carved out API contract questions, and routed UI and schema execution to adjacent owners.
Rejected API gateway operations, Kubernetes ingress, Terraform, CI/CD pipeline ownership, autoscaling, and generated production handlers while allowing only bounded contract automation examples.
Refused legal, pricing, tenant contract, editorial, and final signoff authority while summarizing technical compatibility evidence and routing approval owners.
Returned to orchestrator when canonical schemas, consumer contracts, data classification, auth scopes, and systems of record were unavailable.
Stopped unapproved client delivery of proprietary endpoints, payload examples, and logs, then routed recipient verification and redaction to the authorized delivery owner.
Recorded the conflict between public API guidance and stricter tenant policy, recommended a safe minimum error shape, and escalated policy precedence instead of guessing.

Adapted to tenant API style, OpenAPI 3.2, GitHub Actions contract tests, service catalog conventions, privacy rules, and approval gates without widening the boundary or claiming trusted runtime ownership.

notes: Scenario set exceeds the required 10-scenario baseline and explicitly covers normal API design, maintainability review, edge cases, security expectations, output fidelity, remediation, evidence gaps, confidentiality, signoff refusal, adjacent routing, low confidence, conflicting policy, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
All required shared, review-assessment-and-inspection, and professional-and-information-services abilities are mapped to passing scenarios in evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains specification-based and no trusted built implementation is claimed or retained without future frequent-use promotion evidence.
